name: Bazas and Captieux Loop
name_original: Tour entre Bazas et Captieux - Parc Naturel Regional des Landes de Gascogne
description: Cycle 56.3 miles (90.6 km) through the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne, enjoying pine forests and cultural landmarks like Bazas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Retjons traverse the rural landscape of the Landes department, characterized by extensive pine forests and gently undulating terrain. The region offers a network of quiet country roads, suitable for various road cycling abilities. Elevation gains are generally modest, providing accessible routes through agricultural areas and small villages. Best road cycling routes around Retjons The most popular road cycling route is Loop around Roquefort - Parc Naturel Regional des Landes de Gascogne, an easy 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park's characteristic pine forests and open countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bazas and Captieux Loop, a moderate 56.3 miles (90.6 km) path. This route features a longer distance with varied rural scenery and moderate elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Sainte-Foy Church of Gaillères – The church of Bostens loop from Saint-Gor, a 44.7 miles (72.0 km) trail leading through local villages and agricultural land, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Retjons is defined by quiet country roads, extensive pine forests, and open agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Retjons?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;19&lt;/strong&gt; road cycling routes around Retjons, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. This includes 7 easy routes and 12 moderate routes.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Retjons?
A: Yes, Retjons offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956668004/loop-around-roquefort-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around Roquefort - Parc Naturel Regional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.1 miles (43.6 km) long and explores the characteristic pine forests and open countryside with modest elevation gains.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes around Retjons?
A: The road cycling routes around Retjons vary in distance. You can find shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956668004/loop-around-roquefort-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around Roquefort&lt;/a&gt; at 27.1 miles (43.6 km), up to longer, moderate options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955556185/bazas-and-captieux-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas and Captieux Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 56.3 miles (90.6 km).

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Retjons?
A: Road cycling routes around Retjons traverse the rural landscape of the Landes department, characterized by extensive pine forests and gently undulating terrain. You'll ride through agricultural areas and small villages, enjoying quiet country roads and open countryside.

Q: Are most road cycling routes around Retjons circular?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Retjons are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956668004/loop-around-roquefort-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around Roquefort&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955556185/bazas-and-captieux-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas and Captieux Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Retjons?
A: The region's mild climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling in Retjons, with comfortable temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it's advisable to ride earlier in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore local points of interest. For example, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/662142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Pierre Church of Roquefort&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5071648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lugaut Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, or even parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Railway Line of Retjons&lt;/a&gt;, which has been repurposed for leisure.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: While the terrain around Retjons is generally gently undulating, there are moderate routes that offer longer distances and varied rural scenery for more experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955556185/bazas-and-captieux-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas and Captieux Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at over 56 miles, provides a good challenge with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in Retjons?
A: As Retjons is a rural area, parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many routes. Look for public parking areas in towns like Roquefort or Saint-Gor, which are common access points for the cycling network.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Retjons?
A: The komoot community rates the road cycling experience in Retjons highly, with an average score of 2.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet country roads, the extensive pine forests, and the peaceful, open agricultural landscapes that define the region's routes.

Q: Can I find less-trafficked or 'hidden gem' road cycling routes in Retjons?
A: The region around Retjons is known for its network of quiet country roads, making it easy to find less-trafficked routes away from major roads. Many of the routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26263193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastide-d'Armagnac – The church of Bostens loop from Roquefort&lt;/a&gt;, offer a peaceful cycling experience through charming local villages and agricultural land.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the road cycling routes?
A: While the routes primarily traverse rural areas, you will find opportunities for refreshments in the small villages you pass through. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26263165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roquefort – Café de la Paix loop from Saint-Gor&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions a cafe, and towns like Roquefort often have local establishments.
tips: Bazas Cathedral is a Roman Catholic church located in Bazas, Gironde, France. It is a national monument. The exterior is impressive, and if you wanted to stop a bit longer, you can take a look inside.
The Captieux squirrel, wooden statue representative of the Captieux coat of arms. It is on a quiet stretch of road just outside Captieux.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Tour between Bazas and Captieux?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is approximately 90.6 kilometers long and generally takes around 3 hours and 42 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bazas to Captieux cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the Bazas–Captieux Greenway, which is largely accessible and offers a pleasant experience. While the greenway north of Captieux is noted for its easily passable, mostly paved surfaces, you should be prepared for potential variations. Some sections, particularly south of Captieux, are described as being in 'gravel mode,' meaning they might be unpaved and more challenging. Overall, it's a moderate ride requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You can make a detour in Bazas, an ancient fortified town, to explore the impressive Bazas Cathedral, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Further along, near Captieux, you might spot The Squirrel of Captieux. The entire journey is immersed in the serene pine forests of the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, both Bazas and Captieux are towns with public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the start or end points of the greenway within these towns. As the route is protected from car traffic, you'll need to park in designated areas and then cycle onto the greenway.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle between Bazas and Captieux?
faqs.answer: The Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne is enjoyable for cycling during many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for a moderate ride of this length, with less heat than summer and fewer chances of winter chill. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the pine forests well.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on greenways and trails within the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne, provided they are kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay an entrance fee to cycle on the greenways within the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne. The park is open to the public for recreational activities. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding nature protection and private property.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the route I should be aware of regarding surface quality?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the greenway north of Captieux is mostly paved and easily passable, cyclists should be aware that sections south of Captieux are described as being in 'gravel mode.' This means these parts might be unpaved and could require a bike suitable for varied surfaces, or at least an awareness that the ride might be rougher in those areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Voie de Vézelay Ouest, and smaller portions of the Bergerac - Mont-de-Marsan and Bazas - Roquefort routes. It also briefly crosses the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Both Bazas and Captieux offer amenities for cyclists. Bazas, being a historic town, has various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Captieux also provides services for visitors. It's a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially if you're looking for specific types of food or lodging.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is officially rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness. While parts are easily passable and mostly paved, the overall distance of 90.6 km and some unpaved 'gravel mode' sections might be challenging for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience and a reasonable fitness level will find it more enjoyable.
